expectations.text: Recommends FastAPI (or Django REST) given the Python-first team
expectations.evidence: FastAPI recommended with justification. Django REST Framework explicitly considered and rejected as 'heavier than you need'.
PostgreSQL recommended with MongoDB explicitly rejected: 'Your data is relational. Document stores add complexity without benefit at this scale.'

expectations.text: Recommends a managed auth provider for the auth requirement
expectations.evidence: Recommends fastapi-users + JWT + bcrypt — a self-implemented auth solution. No managed provider (Auth0, Clerk, Supabase Auth) recommended. This adds significant implementation burden.

expectations.text: Recommends a managed database service (Supabase, Railway, or Neon)
expectations.evidence: Supabase, Railway, Render, AWS RDS all mentioned as hosting options for PostgreSQL.

expectations.text: Recommends a specific ORM (SQLAlchemy or SQLModel)

expectations.evidence: pip install command provided with all deps, plus project structure layout and quick-start checklist.

expectations.text: Addresses push notifications (e.g., via Firebase FCM or similar)
expectations.evidence: Firebase FCM recommended with firebase-admin SDK and device token storage pattern described.
